fftParticleDetector.description: Detection of gamma radiation to infer elemental composition.
fftParticleDetector.hints: Characteristic gamma-ray signatures used to identify elements and surface materials.

SunInSpaceLow: The sun is putting out so much radiation pressure that you're better off looking elsewhere

KerbinInSpaceLow: The detector pings occasionally. You'd have better luck a wee bit higher up.
KerbinInSpaceHigh: It seems like you might be detecting a few annihilation events.
KerbinSrfLanded: If there were antimatter here, you'd probably see it. As a fireball.

MohoInSpaceLow: Your readout reports that it can't hear much over the roar of the solar wind.
MohoInSpaceHigh: Your readout reports that it can't hear much over the roar of the solar wind.

EveSrfLanded: The thick atmosphere precludes any gamma ray detection.

JoolInSpaceLow: There's a veritable jackpot of 0.511 MeV gamma rays coming from a little higher up!
JoolInSpaceHigh: The detector reports a number of annihilation events. They're all concentrated down towards that greenish planet, just above its lower local space.

LaytheInSpaceLow: It's confirmed - the magnetic curtains surrounding this world have trapped antiprotons here.
LaytheInSpaceHigh: Above the magnetic fields, you're not seeing much gamma flux.
LaytheSrfLanded: The thick atmosphere precludes any gamma ray detection.
